perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees that the Confidential Information will be kept confidential and that Recipient and its Representatives will not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that (i) it may make any disclosure of the Confidential Information to which the Disclosing Party gives its prior written consent, and (ii) any of the Confidential Information may be disclosed to Recipient’s Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction, who are bound by confidentiality obligations to Recipient or agree to keep such Confidential Information confidential to the same extent as Recipient. In any event, Recipient agrees to undertake reasonable precautions to safeguard and protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information, to accept responsibility for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives, and at its sole expense to take all reasonable measures to restrain its Representatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or uses of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees that the Confidential Information will be kept confidential and that Recipient and its Representatives will not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that (i) it may make any disclosure of the Confidential Information to which the Disclosing Party gives its prior written consent. In any event, Recipient agrees to undertake reasonable precautions to safeguard and protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information, to accept responsibility for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives, and at its sole expense to take all reasonable measures to restrain its Representatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or uses of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause (ii) from Section 2, specifically 'any of the Confidential Information may be disclosed to Recipient’s Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction, who are bound by confidentiality obligations to Recipient or agree to keep such Confidential Information confidential to the same extent as Recipient.' which previously allowed disclosure to recipient's representatives, now it explicitly prohibits any disclosure without written consent, making the evaluation of a 'Possible Transaction' difficult or impossible if the recipient cannot internally share information, contradicting the intended purpose outlined in the agreement's early provisions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and for no other purpose. Recipient agrees that the Confidential Information will be kept confidential and that Recipient and its Representatives will not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that (i) it may make any disclosure of the Confidential Information to which the Disclosing Party gives its prior written consent. In any event, Recipient agrees to undertake reasonable precautions to safeguard and protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information, to accept responsibility for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives, and at its sole expense to take all reasonable measures to restrain its Representatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or uses of the Confidential Information. The Disclosing Party understands that Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that may be similar to Disclosing Party’s information. Accordingly, this Agreement will not be construed as an obligation or representation that Recipient will not develop products or systems, or have products or systems developed for it, that compete with the products or systems contemplated by Disclosing Party’s information. In addition, the Disclosing Party agrees that Recipient does not intend to, and will not be obligated to, restrict or segregate the work assignments of personnel who may have been exposed to Disclosing Party’s information.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed disclosure to recipient's representatives for evaluating a possible transaction. The changed text removes this exception, now requiring written consent for any disclosure. The contradicted text states the recipient and its representatives 'shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction'. By removing the exception in the changed text, the recipient is no longer able to share confidential information with representatives for the purpose of evaluation, directly contradicting the section that requires them to use the information for that purpose.

perturbation.original_text: In addition, both parties agree that, without the prior written consent of the other party, neither it nor its Representatives will disclose to any other person the fact that the Confidential Information has been made available to it, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a Possible Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (whether written or oral), including the status thereof (collectively, the “Discussion Information”); provided, however that a party may make such disclosure if, and solely to the extent that, the other party has already done so or such party has received the written opinion of its outside counsel that such disclosure must be made in order that such party not commit a violation of law.
perturbation.changed_text: In addition, both parties agree that, without the prior written consent of the other party, neither it nor its Representatives will disclose to any other person the fact that the Confidential Information has been made available to it, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a Possible Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (whether written or oral), including the status thereof (collectively, the “Discussion Information”); provided, however that a party may make such disclosure if, and solely to the extent that, the other party has already done so.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that allows disclosure upon written opinion of outside counsel to avoid violation of law, Section 3 creates a potential contradiction. If a party is legally compelled to disclose Discussion Information, they are now in breach of contract, even if withholding the information would violate the law. This undermines the legal practicality implied within the agreement and places parties in a conflicting situation regarding their obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that Recipient or any of its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information, Recipient shall provide the Disclosing Party with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. If, in the absence of a protective order or other remedy or the receipt of a waiver by the Disclosing Party, Recipient or any of its Representatives are nonetheless, in the written opinion of counsel, legally compelled to disclose Confidential Information or Discussion Information to any tribunal or else stand liable for contempt or suffer other censure or penalty, Recipient or its Representatives may, without liability hereunder, disclose to such tribunal only that portion of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information which such counsel advises is legally required to be disclosed, provided that Recipient exercises its commercially reasonable efforts to preserve the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and the Discussion Information, including, without limitation, by cooperating with the Disclosing Party to obtain an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded the Confidential Information and the Discussion Information by such tribunal.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 3 removes the provision allowing disclosure of Discussion Information based on a written opinion of outside counsel to avoid violating the law. The contradicted text explicitly allows for disclosure under such circumstances ('If, in the written opinion of counsel, legally compelled to disclose Confidential Information or Discussion Information to any tribunal or else stand liable for contempt or suffer other censure or penalty, Recipient or its Representatives may, without liability hereunder, disclose...'). By removing this exception, the changed text creates a direct contradiction, as a party compelled by law to disclose information would now be in breach of contract under the changed Section 3, while the contradicted text provides a safe harbor for such disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: Following termination of discussions regarding a Possible Transaction, upon the request (which request shall be made within a reasonable time after such termination of discussions) of the Disclosing Party for any reason, Recipient will, at Recipient’s option, either deliver to the Disclosing Party or destroy (which destruction shall be certified in writing by an officer of Recipient) all Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party pursuant hereto and the portion of all other Confidential Information prepared by Recipient or its Representatives which contains Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party; provided, however, that Recipient and its Representatives (i) may each retain one copy of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information for recordkeeping purposes and for the purposes of defending its rights and obligations hereunder and (ii) will not be required to return or destroy -2- any computer or other electronic hardware or systems, to render any electronic data irrecoverable or to disable or otherwise modify any existing electronic data backup procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: Following termination of discussions regarding a Possible Transaction, upon the request (which request shall be made within a reasonable time after such termination of discussions) of the Disclosing Party for any reason, Recipient will deliver to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party pursuant hereto and the portion of all other Confidential Information prepared by Recipient or its Representatives which contains Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party; provided, however, that Recipient and its Representatives (i) may each retain one copy of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information for recordkeeping purposes and for the purposes of defending its rights and obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the Recipient's option to 'destroy' the Confidential Information and also removing clause (ii) regarding the exception for destruction to not include 'any computer or other electronic hardware or systems, to render any electronic data irrecoverable or to disable or otherwise modify any existing electronic data backup procedures.', in Section 5, the Recipient is now obliged to 'deliver' all Confidential Information. If the Recipient destroys the data, now that's a breach of contract. Additionally, they are forced to return any computer or other electronic hardware or systems, render any electronic data irrecoverable or to disable or otherwise modify any existing electronic data backup procedures. This creates contradiction and imposes a potentially unfeasible and costly burden on the recipient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Following termination of discussions regarding a Possible Transaction, upon the request (which request shall be made within a reasonable time after such termination of discussions) of the Disclosing Party for any reason, Recipient will deliver to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party pursuant hereto and the portion of all other Confidential Information prepared by Recipient or its Representatives which contains Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party; provided, however, that Recipient and its Representatives (i) may each retain one copy of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information for recordkeeping purposes and for the purposes of defending its rights and obligations hereunder. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the option for the Recipient to 'destroy' Confidential Information and removes the exception regarding electronic hardware and data. The contradicted text, which is virtually identical to the changed text, now obligates the Recipient to 'deliver' all Confidential Information. This creates a direct conflict because the original text allowed for destruction, and the current version does not, while still binding the Recipient to obligations of confidentiality. This contradicts what was originally agreed upon.

perturbation.original_text: Both parties understand and agree that no contract or agreement providing for any Possible Transaction shall be deemed to exist between Nuance and the Company unless and until a final definitive agreement has been executed and delivered. Both parties also agree that unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither the Company nor Nuance will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to reject any and all proposals made by the other or any of its Representatives with regard to a Possible Transaction, and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: Both parties understand and agree that no contract or agreement providing for any Possible Transaction shall be deemed to exist between Nuance and the Company unless and until a final definitive agreement has been executed and delivered. Both parties also agree that unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither the Company nor Nuance will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to reject any and all proposals made by the other or any of its Representatives with regard to a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time' from Section 7, it introduces ambiguity regarding the right to unilaterally end negotiations. Although the agreement states that no legal obligation exists until a final agreement is signed, removing the explicit right to terminate discussions could imply that, after a certain point, a party may be obligated to continue negotiating in good faith, particularly if significant resources have been invested based on preliminary agreements or understandings. This creates uncertainty because it contradicts the earlier statement that no obligation exists until a final agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties understand and agree that no contract or agreement providing for any Possible Transaction shall be deemed to exist between Nuance and the Company unless and until a final definitive agreement has been executed and delivered. Both parties also agree that unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither the Company nor Nuance will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to reject any and all proposals made by the other or any of its Representatives with regard to a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that each party reserves the right to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time. The changed text removes this explicit right, potentially implying that parties might be obligated to continue negotiations under certain circumstances. This contradicts the overall agreement that no legal obligation exists until a final definitive agreement is signed, as stated in the contradicted text. The removal creates ambiguity where there was certainty, leading to a potential conflict.

perturbation.original_text: It is further understood and agreed that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or any of its Representatives and that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including an injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by the Recipient of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: It is further understood and agreed that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or any of its Representatives and that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including an injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by the Recipient of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Disclosing Party' from Section 10, the contract language could now be interpreted as limiting the remedies available to the Disclosing Party solely to equitable relief (injunction and specific performance). If money damages are indeed provable and appropriate, the removal of the availability of all other remedies contradicts the implied fairness and comprehensiveness typically sought in contractual agreements, creating a contradiction about how breaches should be addressed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is further understood and agreed that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or any of its Representatives and that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including an injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the phrase 'Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by the Recipient of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Disclosing Party'. This omission creates a contradiction because without that phrase, the agreement implies that the Disclosing Party is only entitled to equitable relief (injunction and specific performance). This contradicts the contradicted text by limiting the available remedies, and contradicting the potential availability of other remedies such as money damages, therefore forming an in-text contradiction.
